hero.question: Hornets vs. Nets
hero.prob_take: The Hornets are priced at 93.5%, which means the market has essentially closed the book on a Brooklyn loss, this is where you come if you want near-certainty, not edge. At this price you're paying heavy for what's likely a talent mismatch, but flat volume at $602K suggests the sharps aren't interested in either side, which should make you pause before chasing certainty in a single NBA game.

daily_take.headline: Hornets-Nets at 93.5% Should Scare You. Here's Why.
daily_take.deck: The market has locked Hornets at 93.5% with essentially zero movement over 24 hours on $602K volume. That flatness is the real signal. A heavily favored team priced this high with no sharp action means one of two things: either the model is right and there's no edge, or the market has already sucked in all the casual money and the real sharps have abandoned it. Check the line movement. If there's been no attempt to knock this down from 93.5%, assume the money knows something. The play is either to fade a market this top-heavy or sit out entirely.
